hero.description: The primary 'above-the-fold' section of the landing page
hero.meta_description: The most critical conversion section. Includes a punchy H1 headline that focuses on a 'Stop [Problem]. Start [Solution]' framework. The description should be technical yet benefit-driven. Includes primary and secondary call-to-action buttons. AI should focus on high-energy, authoritative language.

headline.meta_description: The main H1. Should use a bold, two-part structure (e.g., 'Stop just storing content. Start coordinating your business.'). Max 12 words.

subheadline.meta_description: A 2-3 sentence paragraph explaining the 'How' and 'Who'. Mention target audience (e.g., 'high-performance service providers') and key technical differentiators.

media.meta_description: A high-fidelity visual representing the product. For technical products, this should be an abstract 'aura' graphic, a dashboard preview, or a code-snippet visualization.

label.meta_description: Action-oriented text like 'Get Started' or 'Install Now'.
Lower-friction action like 'View on GitHub' or 'Book Demo'.

valueProps.description: A grid of core feature/benefit cards
valueProps.meta_description: A set of 3 cards highlighting the 'Engineered for...' philosophy. Each card should focus on a distinct pillar (e.g., Design, Commerce, Developer Experience). AI should generate an icon name, a title, and 3 specific bullet points for each.

icon.meta_description: Lucide icon name that semantically matches the pillar (e.g., 'zap', 'shield', 'code').

title.meta_description: Short, punchy pillar title (e.g., 'Agentic by Design').

bulletPoints.meta_description: Exactly 3 bullet points per card. Each should start with a strong verb or adjective and highlight a specific technical capability.

technicalSpecs.description: A structured table for technical transparency and trust
technicalSpecs.meta_description: A table layout intended for 'Reliable Enterprise Scaling'. Lists technical capabilities against specific technologies. AI should populate this with industry-standard stacks relevant to the product (e.g., Edge-native, SQL, React).

specification.meta_description: The specific technology or standard used (e.g., 'MCP / REST / GraphQL').

supportedBy.meta_description: The provider or underlying engine (e.g., 'Native Agent Communication', 'Supabase').

closingCTA.description: The final conversion block at the bottom of the page
closingCTA.meta_description: A high-contrast section designed to catch 'scrollers'. It should reiterate the product's identity in 3 words (e.g., 'Open Source. Edge Native. Agent Ready.') and provide a final invitation to join the ecosystem.

title.meta_description: A 3-word summary of the product's USP.

description.meta_description: A final 1-2 sentence pitch emphasizing the 'Future' or 'Community' aspect.

faq.description: Standard accordion for common technical hurdles
faq.meta_description: Addresses technical objections (Pricing, Open Source license, Integration). AI should generate 3-5 questions that a CTO or Lead Developer would ask.
